signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: FC Bayern Munich, one of the world’s best-known sports clubs, has a long history in basketball dating back to 1946. Bayern made its European debut in the early rounds of the 1968-69 Cup Winners’ Cup. After a few turbulent decades, 2008 saw Bayern purchase the license of the Dusseldorf Magics to return to the German second tier. Bayern went back to the German top flight in 2011 and also received a wild card to play the 2011-12 EuroCup, before legendary coach Svetislav Pesic stepped in ahead of the 2012-13 season. Everything clicked in 2013-14 as Bayern shined in its EuroLeague debut season by reaching the Top 16 and taking the German title for the first time since 1955. The appointment of charismatic Italian coach Andrea Trinchieri in 2020 sparked another surge as Bayern enjoyed its best-ever season on the continental stage, making the EuroLeague Playoffs and forcing EA7 Emporio Armani Milan to five games in a thrilling series. Bayern was one win away from the Final Four in 2022, when it lost 3-2 to FC Barcelona. Two-time EuroLeague champion Pablo Laso arrived as head coach in 2023 and led Bayern to the German double. Bayern, however, missed the EuroLeague postseason for a second straight year. In the 2024-25 season, new coach Gordie Herbert led Bayern to the EuroLeague Play-In Showdown and its seventh German League crown.

heroMedia.title: Swider’s buzzer-beater seals Efes’s dramatic comeback in Munich
heroMedia.summary: The Turkish side’s second-half charge was sparked by Jordan Loyd

linkedIds.text: FC Bayern Munich v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-03-12T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
Anadolu Efes Istanbul pulled off a thrilling 80-81 road win over FC Bayern Munich, capped by a buzzer-beater from Cole Swider that ended the team’s long road losing streak dating back to December and moved its record to 10-21.

Swider had been on the floor for just 3 minutes, but when the coach called his number, he delivered. “Pablo called on me and I try to keep this fireman’s mentality whenever I'm called on, whenever there's a fire, I try and put it out in any sense,’’ said Swider.

‘‘The guys did such a great job coming back in the second half, they deserve the interview, but, yeah, it felt great to win with the team.’’

Efes struggled to find its rhythm in the first half, with Bayern controlling the pace, building a 13-point lead, and exploiting defensive gaps. The visitors got scoring from just six players and had trouble organizing offensively and moving the ball, committing 9 turnovers in the first half.

However, the second half told a different story for Efes. Led by Jordan Loyd, Efes found a better offensive rhythm and gradually clawed its way back to close the gap. After scoring just 26 points in the first half, the visitors racked up 55 in the second, turning the momentum in their favor.

After a quiet first half with 5 points, Jordan Loyd erupted for 15 points in the last 20 minutes, driving Efes’s second-half comeback.

‘‘This group of guys, we’ve been through so much this year,’’ said Loyd after the game. ‘‘And to be able to play some good basketball in the second half and put together a win on the road is huge for us. We’re trying to be as positive as can be, no matter the result, and I’m just super happy for the club.’’

After hitting just 3-of-13 from deep in the first half, Efes found its range in the second, knocking down 9 three-pointers and finishing 44.4% from deep. PJ Dozier stepped up, scoring 12 points after a scoreless first half, while Sehmus Hazer added 11 to keep Efes in the game.

‘‘I'm very proud of them, as a coach,’’ said Pablo Laso after the game. ‘‘I think our first half was not good. We were even playing worse because we were not making shots, and that was coming down in our energy. I think in the second half we were able to make the same shots, we moved the ball well, we got good looks, good shots, and the game-winners.’’

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Anadolu Efes Spor Kulübü (@anadoluefessk)

Bayern, which had controlled the momentum in the first half, struggled to find its shooting rhythm after the interval and committed turnovers as Efes tightened its defense. Xavier Rathan-Mayes led Bayern with 15 points, while Andreas Obst had a quiet night from deep, hitting just 1-of-7 three pointers to finish with 13 points.

‘‘Two different games tonight. First half, excellent game for us. And second, offense, defense, not on the level how we wanted to play, and we didn't find solutions in offense, especially against very aggressive defense from Efes. We didn't react very good after the execution, and they used this,’’ said Coach Svetislav Pesic after the game.

linkedId.text: FC Bayern Munich v Anadolu Efes Istanbul (2026-03-12T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Swider’s buzzer-beater seals Efes’s dramatic comeback in Munich
articleMetadata.description: Anadolu Efes Istanbul pulled off a thrilling 80-81 road win over FC Bayern Munich, capped by a buzzer-beater from Cole Swider that ended the team’s long road losing streak dating back to December and moved its record to 10-21.

heroMedia.title: Andi Obst seals new long-term contract at Bayern
heroMedia.summary: The German sharpshooter will remain in Munich

content.text:
[FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fc-bayern-munich/mun/) has announced that star guard [Andi Obst](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/andreas-obst/004508/) has signed a new contract with the club through the end of the 2028-29 season.

Obst is enjoying a career year for Bayern, averaging 15.5 points per game across 27 EuroLeague appearances this season. All five of his EuroLeague campaigns, starting in 2021-22, have been spent with Bayern.

"I am very, very happy to be able to stay at Bayern, because Munich has also become my home over the last five years – I just feel very comfortable here," Obst told Bayern's official club media. "The conditions at Bayern are perfect for me, so I didn't have to think twice. We still have a lot to do and I'm not done here yet; I want to achieve even more with this club. Bayern has now done everything possible to keep me, which I really appreciate and am grateful for the great trust they have placed in me. That's why it was a very quick decision made with my heart. Because what am I actually missing here? Nothing at all. I hope that I can continue to take on a lot of responsibility for the club; that's my goal."

Es bleibt Obst im Haus! ❤️🤍

Der Champion bleibt zuhause: Andreas Obst verlängert beim FC Bayern Basketball bis 2029: https://t.co/3L5X6d3xFT#Obst2029 #FCBB @FCBayern @Fruit_96 pic.twitter.com/uHOcawFkmG

— FC Bayern Basketball (@FCBBasketball) March 9, 2026

"As everyone can imagine, we are very proud and happy that Andi Obst is staying with us. He is the face of our basketball team and has long been part of the Bayern family," said Bayern club president Herbert Hainer. "In our opinion, Andi is not only the best long-range shooter in Europe, but also more than just an outstanding basketball player thanks to his character. We made it clear to him that the club has big goals in basketball – and so does Andi. The fact that he quickly decided to stay with FC Bayern shows that it was certainly not the most lucrative offer that tipped the scales for him – that speaks volumes about him and our relationship with him. He has found a home here, which is worth a lot. This is a great day for the entire FC Bayern."

Known predominantly for his three-point shooting, [Obst set a EuroLeague record with 11 three-pointers against FC Barcelona](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/news/andreas-obst-breaks-the-euroleague-single-game-record-for-three-pointers-made/) on November 22, 2024. He has made five or more threes six times this season, including [nine against Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/andi-obst-after-shining-against-zvezda-bad-shots-dont-really-exist-for-me/) in Round 2, eight in a road game at Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv in Round 28, and [seven against Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/news/andreas-obst-drops-37-as-bayern-stuns-baskonia-with-epic-comeback/) in Round 20.

The 29-year-old has added more elements to his game this season, making a career-best 49 two-pointers and drawing a career-high 78 fouls, while he is on track to set new personal-best marks in rebounds and assists. His combined PIR of 347 this season is also a career best.

Prior to joining Bayern in 2021, Obst became familiar with continental fans after making a positive impact with ratiopharm Ulm in the BKT EuroCup during the 2019-20 and 2020-21 seasons, averaging double digits in points each of those seasons. He made his EuroCup debut with Brose Bamberg in 2014-15.

Obst has been a German League champion twice, winning in 2016 with Bamberg and in 2024 with Bayern. He also won the German Cup with Bayern in 2023 and 2024.

articleMetadata.title: Andi Obst seals new long-term contract at Bayern
articleMetadata.description: FC Bayern Munich has announced that star guard Andi Obst has signed a new contract with the club through the end of the 2028-29 season.

heroMedia.title: Bayern stuns Crvena Zvezda on the road with huge defensive effort
heroMedia.summary: The German side held the home team to just 6-of-30 from deep as striking points endured a torrid shooting night

linkedIds.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v FC Bayern Munich (2026-03-06T19:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
[FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/fc-bayern-munich/mun/) ground out a well-deserved 80-85 win at [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/) to keep alive its slim hopes of reaching the postseason while denting the home side’s playoff hopes after forcing the final twist in a titanic tussle on Friday.

Bayern bounced back from failure in the German Cup and a crushing defeat at Real Madrid in the previous round to subdue Zvezda, improving to 13-17 while the Serbian team dropped to 17-13 after shooting a dreadful 6-of-30 from downtown.

Bayern firmly held the upper hand in the opening half and led by as many as 16 points (20-36) as the visiting team’s airtight defense gave Zvezda preciously little room to operate. Zvezda made its opening three from its 10th attempt as sharpshooters [Jordan Nwora](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jordan-nwora/013363/) and [Codi Miller-McIntyre](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/codi-miller-mcintyre/009048/) struggled all night, making a combined 1-of-11 from long range while [Neno Dimitrijevic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/neno-dimitrijevic/006085/) could not put a foot wrong at the other end of the court.

content.title: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade-FC Bayern Munich | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Still, Zvezda came back to life after a 7-0 run, coupled with an audacious triple from [Jared Butler](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jared-butler/014163/) on the halftime buzzer, slashing the deficit to 43-50 at the interval to pump up the volume in the stands. The hosts then nosed ahead 68-67 late in the third quarter and having beefed up its defense in the fourth, Zvezda carved out a 78-75 advantage heading into the home stretch.

The Serbian side went ice-cold from the floor, however, when the chips were down and Bayern forced the final twist on the back of a 2-10 run with [Xavier Rathan-Mayes](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/xavier-rathan-mayes/013378/) and [Andi Obst](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/andreas-obst/004508/) keeping their nerve to silence a noisy home crowd in the Belgrade Arena.      

“It was very intense from the off and the crowd here always makes it difficult to get into it,” Obst said in his postgame comments. “We managed to keep our cool after Red Star made some runs and in the end we deserved to win. We kept fighting for every ball and the bench did a pretty good job. That’s what this team is about as anybody can make an impact,” added the German international guard, who netted 16 points despite shooting a poor 2-of-8 from deep.

However, Obst found other ways to score from inside and played hard defense which frustrated Zvezda while Dimitrijevic added 11 points, 4 rebounds and as many assists. Rathan-Mayes garnished a sound performance with 13 points and [David McCormack](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/david-mccormack/013386/) chipped in with 10 points and 4 boards for Bayern, which shot 11-of-28 from deep.

“We were coming from a difficult moment after losing in the German Cup and we also played well below par against Real Madrid,” Dimitrijevic said. “We stuck together as a team and that’s our biggest strength. We got some key stops and we’re going to fight until the end to get into those play-in spots.”

X stepped up tonight. 😮‍💨 #CZVBAY pic.twitter.com/Gp35HS9tuk

— FC Bayern Basketball (@FCBBasketball) March 6, 2026

Zvezda head coach Sasa Obradovic was bitterly disappointed with his team’s below-par performance, with the outcome potentially inflicting heavy damage to Zvezda’s bid of getting into the business end of Europe’s premier club competition. 

“This could turn out to be a costly defeat but it’s not easy to play against a good team devoid of pressure,” Obardovic told the postgame press conference. “We didn’t handle the pressure tonight and having struggled offensively, we failed to close shop on defense to make sure way we found a way to win. We just weren’t aggressive enough against a team who knew what they were doing and it cost us in the end.”

[Chima Moneke](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/chima-moneke/012201/) poured in 22 points for a disjointed Zvezda side while Butler and Nwora added 10 apiece, but the hosts ultimately paid the price for missing too many open shots when the chips were down. Bayern head coach Svetsislav Pesic said it was a morale-boosting moment for his outfit, which snapped a two-game losing streak. 

“It will do the team a world of good and even more so than by the result itself, we’re buoyed by the maturity and character we showed tonight,” the veteran Serbian tactician told the Balkan country’s Arena Sport television. “It was a close game and a tough challenge to beat such a strong team as Zvezda on the road, so credit to the lads for pulling off this one.”

linkedId.text: Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade v FC Bayern Munich (2026-03-06T19:00:00.000Z)

heroMedia.title: Real Madrid crushes Bayern to reset its rhythm
heroMedia.summary: Sergio Scariolo’s side restores control on both ends, rolling by 23 as Mario Hezonja led a balanced attack

linkedIds.text: Real Madrid v FC Bayern Munich (2026-02-26T19:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
“If you look at the standings, it’s an extremely important win, but especially for the way we did it together, with all the players contributing and with good team basketball,” said Sergio Scariolo, moments after his [Real Madrid](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/real-madrid/mad/) team cruised to a 93-70 victory over [FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fc-bayern-munich/mun/).

The Italian head coach went as far as suggesting it was something as a statement win for his team, after a brutally difficult weekend. “I am very, very pleased by the way the players reacted together, playing defense, sharing the ball and probably for three quarters, putting on one of our best performances of the season.”

It was a much-needed performance too, as Real Madrid entered the game having won just one of its last four EuroLeague games. *Los Blancos* also missed out on the chance for some silverware on Sunday when it lost to Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz in the Spanish Cup final, giving it an extra point to prove ahead of this one.

content.title: Real Madrid-FC Bayern Munich | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
The hosts set the tone early in the first quarter, attacking the paint to establish a lead and finish the opening period ahead by 7 points. The Spanish side was relentless as the first half developed, building upon the advantage to widen the gap to 20 points at halftime.

It was much of the same in the second half, as Real Madrid punished the Germans on both ends to move ahead by 31. Despite Bayern’s best efforts, the difference was insurmountable and when the final buzzer sounded, it had fallen to a 23-point loss, sliding to 12-17 for the season. Real Madrid, on the other hand, strengthens its EuroLeague record to 18-11 and bounces back to winning ways.

[Mario Hezonja](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mario-hezonja/003108/) led the Spanish giants with 16 points. His 19 PIR led the way for both teams and he achieved it in under 20 minutes of play. The Croatian forward was encouraged by the character his team showed after such a disappointing period.

“It’s a ridiculous situation what happened last week,” Hezonja stated, referring to the cup loss. “We were very angry and frustrated, but as a professional, you have a couple of days to think about what happened, but you have to put your head up, be professional and try to win the next game.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Real Madrid Basketball (@realmadridbasket)

Adding to Bayern’s pain, [Trey Lyles](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/trey-lyles/014117/) scored 12 and [Walter Tavares](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/walter-tavares/005791/) 11. Tavares shared Hezonja’s sentiment.

“We have to try to forget the past and keep moving,” Tavares said. “We had a tough loss in the final and feel like we could have done better, but we have to stay focused, keep moving. The most important thing is going to the Final Four, as we have a great team and have to keep our heads up.”

Bayern’s [Justinian Jessup](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/justinian-jessup/013290/) logged 17 points in defeat. Ultimately, it was a night to forget for Bayern, which shot 6-21 from three-point range. Despite having won four of its last five EuroLeague contests, Svetislav Pesic’s men came into the clash as clear underdogs. According to the Serbian legend, meeting a resurgent Real Madrid at this specific point of the season made it especially difficult.

“This game comes in a difficult moment, because Madrid didn’t play like people expect for them to win the Copy del Rey,” said Pesic. “On our side, we were in a difficult situation, because some players are sick or have physical problems. I can’t say that if everything was perfect, we would win the game, but this is the real situation.”

Pesic and his team will get a chance to bounce back next Friday, when they face Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade on the road. Madrid will hope to carry some momentum into their meeting with Virtus Bologna on Thursday.

linkedId.text: Real Madrid v FC Bayern Munich (2026-02-26T19:45:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Real Madrid crushes Bayern to reset its rhythm
articleMetadata.description: Real Madrid rebounded from its Spanish Cup disappointment with a 93–70 home win over Bayern Munich, controlling the game from the opening quarter and leading by 20 at halftime.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: German Cup: Bayern ousted in semis OT thriller

content.text:
FC Bayern Munich did not manage to use home floor to its advantage, finishing on the losing end of an overtime thriller against Bamberg, which celebrated a 97-103 win in Saturday’s first semifinal.

Bamberg used a 4-17 run to open 72-78 lead in the fourth quarter, and Bayern was down 77-83 in the final minute. But Andy Obst hit a catch-and-shoot triple with 48 seconds left, then Neno Dimitrijevic made a second-chance three-pointer 11.3 second before the end of regulation, tying it at 83-83. In the remaining time, Bayern made a defensive stand, not even allowing Bamberg to take a shot, and the game went into overtime.

The extra session saw two teams combine to make seven triples on seven consecutive trips down the flow. Obst hit a step-back to tie it at 90-90, Oscar Da Silva made it 93-93, Dimitrijevic’s triple tied it at 96-96 with 1:05 left on the clock. But Ibi Watson hit a corner three to give Bamberg 96-99, and Bayern did not manage to come back from that deficit.

Watson paced Bamberg with 24 points, 7 assists and 6 rebounds, while Cobe Williams had 22 points and 10 assists in a victory. Dimitrijevic led Bayern with 24 points, Obst and Vladimir Lucic scored 17 apiece, and Isaia Mikea collected 13 points and 12 rebounds in a loss.

articleMetadata.title: German Cup: Bayern ousted in semis OT thriller

heroMedia.title: Maccabi breaks club record, outguns Bayern in OT classic
heroMedia.summary: A club-record 20 triples and a EuroLeague-record 35 combined threes powered Maccabi to another home win

linkedIds.text: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv v FC Bayern Munich (2026-02-12T19:05:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/maccabi-rapyd-tel-aviv/tel/) remained undefeated in games at its home fortress, Menora Mivtachim Arena, this season by winning another thriller. This time it was 111-106 after overtime against [FC Bayern Munich](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/fc-bayern-munich/mun/) as Maccabi set a club record with 20 three-pointers and the two teams tied a EuroLeague record with 35 combined triples.

Both teams’ head coaches walked out satisfied with how their players performed, which served as a testament to the high level of play.

“Crazy shots, big shots,” Maccabi head coach Oded Kattash summed up the shooting. “It was really tough, it could have gone either way. We could not have done it without our fans. I am happy.”

Kattash went on to say, “I don’t think the effort on defense matches the number of points scored” as he highlighted the efforts of [Oshae Brissett](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/o-shae-j-brissett/014127/) and [Will Rayman](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/william-rayman/013381/) on Bayern star [Andreas Obst](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/andreas-obst/004508/), who finished with 33 points.

content.title: Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv-FC Bayern Munich | Round 28 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Maccabi led throughout the first quarter, which ended 26-20. [Gur Lavy](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/gur-lavi/014120/) ignited the crowd with the first 5 points of the second quarter to open a double-digit lead.

Kattash called the way Maccabi played in the first 14 minutes “perfect,” but credited Bayern for what happened next. “We relaxed for 2-3 minutes and Obst, who is in the best shape of his career, led them and they scored like 15 points in 2-3 minutes.”

The game was close the rest of the way. Both teams had chances to win it late. [Neno Dimitrijevic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/neno-dimitrijevic/006085/), who tallied 15 points and 10 assists, made a tough three – and the foul shot that came with it – to give Bayern a 98-99 lead with 9.6 seconds left. After two timeouts, Maccabi gave the ball to [Jimmy Clark](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/clark-iii-jimmy/013699/), the hero of its last two wins, and he found an open Brissett under the basket. Brissett was fouled, split the free throws and the game went to overtime. Clark started the overtime strong and [Jaylen Hoard](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jaylen-hoard/006835/) chipped in before Brissett all but sealed the win with a steal and slam.

The win lifted Maccabi’s record to 13-15 and dropped Bayern to 12-16.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Maccabi Tel Aviv Basketball (@maccabitlv)

It was a balanced scoring display by Maccabi with six scorers in double figures. [Roman Sorkin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/roman-sorkin/011219/) led the way with 21 points, [Tamir Blatt](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tamir-blatt/011193/) shined with 20 points on 6-of-6 three-point shooting plus 9 assists, Rayman was perfect on 5 three-point tries for a career-high 16 points, Hoard finished with 14 points and 11 rebounds, [Iffe Lundberg](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/iffe-lundberg/010781/) added 13 points, and Clark netted 10.

“That was unbelievable. I’ve never experienced this kind of shooting display from a team, Rayman said after the game.

Obst paced Bayern with 33 points, [David McCormack](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/david-mccormack/013386/) paired 17 points with 8 boards, Dimitrijevic tallied 15 points and 10 assists, and Niels Giffey also scored 15. The visitors were without [Vladimir Lucic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/vladimir-lucic/001288/), [Stefan Jovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/stefan-jovic/004863/) and [Oscar da Silva](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/oscar-da-silva/011244/), in addition to long-term absentee [Kamar Baldwin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kamar-baldwin/012597/).

Coach Svetislav Pesic called it an “excellent game for the fans, excellent game for my players. Of course we are not happy, but we are satisfied with how we played. We had a chance in regular time to win the game…  I cant say anything negative about my team. They fought until the end.”
